channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Zelensky ousts popular defense minister, an architect of Ukraine’s drone program
item.dc:creator: Serhiy Morgunov, David Stern
item.description: Mykhailo Fedorov's dismissal, part of a rebooting of the government announced Sunday, prompted protests in Kyiv.

item.title: Problematic paragraph on Strait of Hormuz places strain on U.S.-Iran deal
item.dc:creator: Susannah George
item.description: Ambiguous language on who controls the key choke point has led to escalating conflict.

item.title: Iran threatens region’s infrastructure as U.S. strikes expand
item.dc:creator: Rachel Chason, Suzan Haidamous, Mohamad El Chamaa
item.description: Rhetoric sharpened on both sides after days of renewed attacks, with Iranian state media reporting strikes around Tehran.

item.title: Iran releases U.S. citizen in goodwill gesture, Trump says
item.dc:creator: Victoria Craw
item.description: Dena Karari had been trapped in Iran since December 2024, her attorney said. Several other Americans are believed to still be in Iranian custody.

item.title: Snipers, warships and Europe’s escalating fight against cocaine smugglers
item.dc:creator: Greg Miller
item.description: A French attack that disabled a drug-smuggling boat in the Atlantic opened a new front, but European proposals stop short of the Trump administration’s more aggressive tactics.

item.title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes after Trump threatens wider attack
item.dc:creator: Rachel Chason
item.description: The latest round of attacks comes amid continued threats by Trump to “knock out” Iran’s power plants and bridges unless Tehran returns to the negotiating table.

item.title: Police round up Hong Kong booksellers as China widens crackdown
item.dc:creator: Joshua Yang
item.description: After pro-democracy protests roiled the city in 2019, Beijing cracked down on freedom of speech, and restrictions have continued to tighten.

item.title: To quell graft allegations, Nigel Farage runs against a man wearing a trash can
item.dc:creator: Steve Hendrix
item.description: The British ally of President Donald Trump wanted a vote of confidence. Instead, other parties dumped their campaigns, and a comedian stepped in.
